title: The Story of the Rome, Watertown, and Ogdensburg Railroad
author: Hungerford, Edward, 1875-1948

quoteFirst: We were most fortunate in having a cool, grey morning; and after sundry delays, at which Indians are adepts, we started off from Kamaiwâwong at
quoteLast: Our party consisted of Schoolmaster and twelve other Arekunas, some to act as baggage-carriers and some to cut open a trail where the ascent was through forest.
title: Through British Guiana to the summit of Roraima
author: Clementi, Cecil, Mrs., 1889-1970
